children.heading: Human consumption
children.label: 1 Human consumption
children.text: The term “human consumption”, when used in the context of a reference to sugar (whether in the form of sugar, in-process sugar, syrup, molasses, or in some other form) for human consumption, includes sugar for use in human food, beverages, or similar products.

children.heading: Mainland State
children.label: 2 Mainland State
children.text: The term “mainland State” means a State other than an offshore State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “market” means to sell or otherwise dispose of in commerce in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: the forfeiture of sugar under the loan program for sugar established under ;section 7272 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to any integrated processor and refiner, the movement of raw cane sugar into the refining process; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the sale of sugar for the production of ethanol or other bioenergy product, if the disposition of the sugar is administered by the Secretary under .section 8110 of this title

children.heading: Marketing year
children.label: C Marketing year
children.text: Forfeited sugar described in subparagraph (B)(i) shall be considered to have been marketed during the crop year for which a loan is made under the loan program described in that subparagraph.

children.heading: Offshore State
children.label: 4 Offshore State
children.text: The term “offshore State” means a sugarcane producing State located outside of the continental United States.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 6 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.
